reason: Apply the required shared cross-layer and reuse thinking guides before changing shared filesystem, settings, IPC, and archive mechanisms.
Reuse the existing NT/openat/identity, sanitizer, budget, and settings primitives instead of adding parallel security implementations.

Apply root Rust backend quality requirements and preserve the established common-gate behavior.
Apply the complete root cross-layer pre-development and generated-binding quality checklist.
Implement handle-bound history reads/writes, pre-IPC hydration budgets, and bounded secret-free generation failures.

Treat flowId as a bearer capability and preserve OAuth ownership, cancellation, and secret-free diagnostics.
Apply lock-owned field mutation and unrelated-field preservation patterns to all production settings writers.
Preserve user-selected option A common-gate skipped/continue/full-503 semantics and forbid DeniedByCircuit early removal.
Carry forward the explicit option A product decision while closing round-4 findings.
Apply field-owned locked RMW, whole-snapshot CAS, and concurrency-safe rollback to every production settings writer.
Implement exact self-reference rewriting and full JSONL validation before archive auto-commit.
Keep the shipped settings ownership code-spec template synchronized with the executable project contract.
Keep the shipped Trellis archive code-spec template synchronized with the executable project contract.
